heroHeadline: CJNG Internal War Week 2: 'El Salo' Plaza Chief Arrested in Tecate as Cartel-Sinaloa War Expands to Baja California
heroSubtitle: Day 56 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco. The fractura total — El Jardinero/El Sapo vs. O3/Los Cuinis (declared April 11) — enters its second full weekend with both factions in a buildup phase: no direct inter-faction combat in the Jalisco heartland confirmed. The week's most significant new enforcement development: the arrest of Salomón Barragán Castañeda ('El Salo,' 35) on April 17 in Tecate, Baja California — a CJNG plaza chief who defected from the Sinaloa Cartel in December 2025 and was linked to 80+ forced disappearances and 30+ homicides in just four months. El Salo's rapid violence trajectory illustrates how post-Mencho fragmentation is fueling accelerated local conflicts as Sinaloa Cartel defectors recruit into CJNG cells across new territories. In San Luis Potosí, 9 CJNG suspects including cell leader 'Flako' arrested in Villa de Reyes (April 16). Operativo Enjambre cumulative total exceeds 80 officials detained. Rancho Izaguirre forensic investigation continues; 95–98 DNA profiles obtained, 2 victims ID'd. ACLED scheduled April 23 webinar on CJNG succession dynamics. FIFA World Cup 2026 opening match: 53 days away (June 11). Guadalajara's Akron Stadium hosts four group-stage matches.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, U.S. Treasury (OFAC), InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, Wall Street Journal, El País, Washington Post, France 24, CGTN, Al Jazeera, Latin Times, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, WOLA, Milenio, Excélsior, Proceso, El Financiero, El Imparcial, Borderland Beat, RED Michoacán, Semanario ZETA, La Voz de la Frontera, TV Azteca Baja California, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: April 19, 2026.
